title: Helping a newcomer
desc: Pass on what you were once given
setting: In the supermarket you notice someone new to the country struggling to be understood at the till. You step in.

steps.speaker: Du
steps.es: (You see them turning red, stuck. How do you step in?)
steps.en: (You see them turning red, stuck. How do you step in?)

options.es: Cha ich öppis hälfe? Ich han das au mal dur.
options.en: Can I help with something? I've been through this too.
options.feedback: Warm, humble, and instantly disarming — exactly the hand you once wished for.

options.es: Sie bruuchid en offizielle Sack für de Güsel.
options.en: You need an official bag for the rubbish.
options.feedback: Helpful info, but lead with warmth — they're flustered, not just uninformed.
You of all people know how that moment feels. A small word would mean the world.

steps.speaker: Nöichömling
steps.es: Oh, ja bitte! Ich verstah nöd, was si vo mir wott.
steps.en: Oh, yes please! I don't understand what she wants from me.

options.es: Si fragt nur, öb Sie e Tüte wänd. Säged eifach ja oder nei.
options.en: She's just asking if you want a bag. Just say yes or no.
options.feedback: Clear, calm, and you shrink the scary moment down to something doable. Lovely.

options.es: Kei Sorg, das isch nüt Schlimms.
options.feedback: Reassuring — and then a quick translation gets them unstuck.

options.en: That's just Swiss German for you.
options.feedback: True, but it doesn't solve their immediate problem — translate the actual question.

steps.speaker: Nöichömling
steps.es: Aaah, e Tüte! Merci. Si redet eifach so schnäll.
steps.en: Aaah, a bag! Thanks. She just talks so fast.

options.es: Ich weiss, am Aafang tönt alls wie eis Wort. Das wird besser.
options.en: I know, at the start it all sounds like one word. It gets better.
options.feedback: You name the exact feeling and offer hope — the encouragement you once needed.

options.es: Mit de Ziit gwöhnt mer sich dra, versproche.
options.en: With time you get used to it, I promise.
options.feedback: Kind and true — a small promise from someone who made it through.

options.en: Yeah, she really does talk too fast.
options.feedback: Sympathetic, but lift them up rather than just agreeing it's hard.

steps.speaker: Nöichömling
steps.es: Danke, ehrlich. Es isch schön, dass öpper hilft.
steps.en: Thank you, honestly. It's nice that someone helps.

options.es: Gärn gscheh. Öpper het mir au mal ghulfe. Viel Glück da!
options.en: You're very welcome. Someone once helped me too. Good luck here!
options.feedback: You close the circle out loud — the moment you've been working toward for years.

options.es: Kei Ursach. Sie schaffed das scho.
options.en: No problem. You'll manage just fine.
options.feedback: Warm and confidence-giving — a perfect send-off.

options.es: Scho guet, isch ja nüt gsii.
options.feedback: It wasn't nothing to them — let yourself accept the thanks.
